hypothesis: Stale-cycle runner hardening becomes safer if runner-stability cards can prove recovery only with an explicit root-relative evidence-command boundary instead of mixed arbitrary shell steps.
failure_mode: A stale-cycle card can currently include arbitrary non-runner commands inside evidence_commands as long as one replayable runner script exists, so the proof trail is not fully bounded or replayable.
closed_fail_rule: Keep the slice only if runner-stability cards fail verification unless every evidence command stays inside the explicit root-relative runner boundary and any `claw:health` observability remains trailing.
measurement: Before the ratchet, `node CLAW/scripts/verify-systems-optimizer.mjs` only required `sysopt.runner-stability-*` cards to include at least one replayable runner script and optional trailing `claw:health`, so arbitrary non-runner commands could still hide inside `evidence_commands` and weaken replayability. After the ratchet, the verifier rejects any runner-stability card whose `evidence_commands` contain anything other than root-relative `node CLAW/scripts/recover-cycle.mjs ...`, `node CLAW/scripts/run-recovery-drill.mjs ...`, and optional trailing `node CLAW/scripts/run-site-script.mjs claw:health`, while the fixed scope-local promotion bundle stays green.
keep_rule: Keep only if runner-stability evidence commands are limited to the explicit root-relative runner boundary plus optional trailing health observability and the fixed scope-local evaluation bundle passes without touching route implementation paths.
learning_item: Runner-stability evidence commands must stay inside an explicit root-relative runner boundary; arbitrary shell steps inside `evidence_commands` weaken replayability and closed-fail recovery proof.
